title: Secondary Index Design for Hybrid Tables
description: Structured reference for designing secondary indexes on Snowflake Hybrid Tables. Optimized for LLM retrieval, code generation, and implementation guidance.
prerequisites: Snowflake paid account in AWS or Azure commercial region
Role with CREATE TABLE on target schema
SELECT privilege on table for adding indexes to existing tables

core_concepts.hybrid_table_storage: Hybrid Tables store data in a row-oriented store. Queries resolve to: (1) ROW_BASED primary-key access, (2) ROW_BASED secondary-index access, or (3) COLUMN_BASED full scan from object storage.

index_types.user_defined: Secondary indexes created with CREATE INDEX or INDEX clause in CREATE HYBRID TABLE

scan_modes.ROW_BASED_TableScan: Primary key lookup. Fast. Single row.
scan_modes.ROW_BASED_IndexScan: Secondary index seek. Fast. Rows proportional to matches.
scan_modes.COLUMN_BASED_TableScan: Full table scan from object storage. Slow. Entire table scanned.

index_design_rules.name: equality_first_range_last
index_design_rules.description: In a composite index, place equality predicate columns first, followed by at most one range predicate column at the end.
index_design_rules.example_good: CREATE INDEX idx ON orders (status, region, created_at)
index_design_rules.example_bad: CREATE INDEX idx ON orders (created_at, status, region)
index_design_rules.reason: The B-tree is sorted by column order. Range predicates break the seek prefix for subsequent columns.

index_design_rules.name: higher_cardinality_leads
index_design_rules.description: Within the equality prefix, place higher-cardinality columns before lower-cardinality columns.
index_design_rules.example_good: CREATE INDEX idx ON orders (region, is_active, created_at)
index_design_rules.example_bad: CREATE INDEX idx ON orders (is_active, region, created_at)
index_design_rules.reason: A boolean leading column still scans ~50% of the index on every seek.

index_design_rules.name: use_include_for_covering
index_design_rules.description: Add INCLUDE columns to avoid probe scans on hot query paths where projected columns are stable.
index_design_rules.syntax: CREATE INDEX idx ON table (key_cols) INCLUDE (projected_cols)
index_design_rules.tradeoff: Increases storage (columns stored in both primary store and index). Only use for high-throughput paths.

index_design_rules.name: match_data_types
index_design_rules.description: Predicate values must match the indexed column's data type. CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() returns TIMESTAMP_LTZ; if the column is TIMESTAMP_NTZ, cast with ::TIMESTAMP_NTZ.
index_design_rules.reason: Implicit type conversion prevents predicate pushdown to the index.

index_design_rules.name: use_session_variables_not_functions
index_design_rules.description: Non-deterministic session functions (CURRENT_USER, CURRENT_SESSION, CURRENT_ACCOUNT, CURRENT_ROLE) cannot be pushed to index access predicates.
index_design_rules.example_good: SET u = CURRENT_USER(); SELECT * FROM t WHERE created_by = $u;
index_design_rules.example_bad: SELECT * FROM t WHERE created_by = CURRENT_USER();
index_design_rules.reason: Runtime-evaluated functions are not known at compile time.

anti_patterns.name: ILIKE
anti_patterns.description: ILIKE never uses an index. Use LIKE with constant prefix or normalize case at write time.

anti_patterns.name: function_on_indexed_column
anti_patterns.description: Wrapping an indexed column in a function (LOWER, UPPER, TRIM, etc.) disqualifies the index.
anti_patterns.fix: Normalize values at write time; query without function.

anti_patterns.name: non_leading_column
anti_patterns.description: Querying on a non-leading column of a composite index cannot use that index.
anti_patterns.fix: Create a separate index with the desired column as the leading key.

anti_patterns.name: range_before_equality
anti_patterns.description: A range predicate on a non-final column in the index breaks the seek prefix for all subsequent columns.
anti_patterns.fix: Reorder: equality predicates first, single range predicate last.

anti_patterns.name: low_cardinality_standalone
anti_patterns.description: An index on a column with very few distinct values provides minimal filtering benefit alone.
anti_patterns.fix: Combine with higher-cardinality columns in a composite index.

anti_patterns.name: non_deterministic_session_function
anti_patterns.description: CURRENT_USER(), CURRENT_SESSION(), CURRENT_ACCOUNT(), CURRENT_ROLE() cannot be pushed to index access predicates.
anti_patterns.fix: Capture value in a session variable first, then use the variable in the predicate.

ddl_reference.create_index_separate: CREATE INDEX idx_name ON table_name (col1, col2);
ddl_reference.create_index_with_include: CREATE INDEX idx_name ON table_name (col1, col2) INCLUDE (col3, col4);
ddl_reference.check_build_status: Run SHOW INDEXES IN TABLE; check the 'status' column. ACTIVE = ready. BUILD IN PROGRESS = still building.

problematic_patterns.meaning: Full table scan. Add secondary index for WHERE clause columns.
Too many row store requests. Index may be non-selective.

decision_tree.question: Is the query filtering on the primary key?
decision_tree.yes: No index needed. PK access is automatic and optimal.

no.question: Does the WHERE clause filter on a specific non-PK column with supported predicates?

yes.question: Is this a single equality predicate?
yes.yes: Create a single-column secondary index: CREATE INDEX idx ON table (col)

yes.action: Create a composite index. Order: equality columns first (higher cardinality leads), range column last.

followup.question: Does the query always project the same small set of columns?
followup.yes: Add INCLUDE for those projected columns to eliminate probe scan.
followup.no: Standard composite index without INCLUDE is sufficient.

no.no: Index cannot help. Consider restructuring the query or normalizing data at write time.

operational_notes.concurrent_builds: Only one index build can run at a time per table. Multiple CREATE INDEX commands queue sequentially.
operational_notes.storage_impact: Each secondary index adds storage. INCLUDE columns increase storage further. Monitor with SHOW HYBRID TABLES.
operational_notes.write_overhead: Indexes are maintained synchronously on every DML. More indexes = higher write latency.
operational_notes.production_best_practice: Use bound variables (parameterized queries) for plan cache reuse. See: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/tables-hybrid-best-practices
